content.title: This work integrates 6 K-BERDL databases + 1 external reference catalog (RefSeq) across 10 notebooks within a single tenant (`beril`).

slides.speaker_notes_seed: RB-TnSeq (198-experiment Caulobacter fitness compendium) and PaperBLAST (literature-grounded functional annotation) are the primary K-BERDL workhorses spanning most notebooks; GO, Pfam, PubMed, and Isolates supply GO term enrichment for the ChvI cohort, domain-level classification of the Fur-released set, literature curation, and strain metadata. RefSeq served as the external reference catalog for the Pfam HMM annotation-presence panel in NB06b that confirmed sphingolipid pathway and ChvG-ChvI restriction to alphaproteobacteria. All 10 notebooks ran entirely within the `beril` tenant; no cross-tenant integration occurred in this study.
